recent_commits.message:
feat: Phase 2 complete — Audit Engine, Action Queue, AI Copilot, Auth

BACKEND
- engines/audit_engine.py: UC Pendency scorer + CAG Audit Risk scorer (0-100)
  with score breakdown (uc_pendency 40%, absorption_gap 25%, leakage 20%, pattern 15%)
  predicts objection type: UC_PENDING, IDLE_FUNDS, RUSH_EXPENDITURE, DIVERSION etc.
- routers/audit.py: GET /api/audit/risk, /uc-pendency, /summary, /objections
- schemas/audit.py: AuditRiskRecord, UCPendencyRecord, AuditSummary, ScoreBreakdown
- engines/action_queue.py: pre-justified fund transfer orders with GFR compliance
  DRAFT→APPROVED/MODIFIED/DISMISSED lifecycle, stable action IDs, policy memo generator
- routers/actions.py: full CRUD + approve/dismiss/memo endpoints
- engines/copilot_engine.py: Groq llama-3.3-70b AI copilot with cross-engine synthesis
  tool-calling architecture — Groq narrates, engines compute, zero hallucination
- routers/copilot.py: /ask (Q&A) + /digest/cached (pre-generated morning briefing)
- engines/realloc_engine.py → replaced by audit_engine.py (removed from all imports)
- schemas/__init__.py + engines/__init__.py: reallocation removed, audit added
- data/generate_data.py: realistic 2023+2024 data — 46.8% leakage in 2023 across
  all 3 flow levels (Centre→State, State→District, District→Dept), mean absorption 83.7%
- fund_releases.csv + expenditures.csv: regenerated with 14,400 rows, 2 years

FRONTEND
- SankeyFlow.tsx: full rewrite from Recharts to D3-Sankey, red leakage edges with
  pulse animation + glow filter, state filter dropdown, tooltip with absorption bar
- graph_engine.py: level normalization (Title Case), NaN-safe helpers _safe_float/_safe_str,
  received derived from absorption_ratio when amount_received=0
- app/(auth)/layout.tsx: isolated auth layout, position:fixed covers dashboard bleed
- app/(auth)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx: centered Clerk card, dark theme,
  removed html/body tags from nested layout
recent_commits.summary: This commit significantly restructures the backend to incorporate AI-driven financial intelligence, focusing on audit risk prediction, action management, and cross-engine synthesis. It introduces new modules like `audit_engine`, `action_queue`, and `copilot_engine` with integrated Groq capabilities. The code is well-architected, integrating multiple services and providing robust functionality for real-time data processing.

final_review:
Bug Buster's team built a robust financial intelligence platform using AI-driven algorithms, focusing on audit risk prediction, action management, and cross-engine synthesis. They refactored the project architecture to Budget Flow Intelligence and created an initial README.md file for the hackathon project. API keys were moved to environment variables, and they introduced new modules like `audit_engine`, `action_queue`, and `copilot_engine` with integrated Groq capabilities. The team significantly restructured the backend, integrating multiple services and providing robust functionality for real-time data processing.

The commit history shows a mix of setup, configuration, feature development, and deployment commits. The code quality was generally good, with scores ranging from 1 to 20. Some commits were well-structured, while others appeared to be merge commits or empty changes. There were no AI-generated portions in the codebase.

The team's strengths included their ability to refactor and improve the project architecture, as well as their strong focus on real-time data processing and AI-driven financial intelligence. Their weaknesses included some wasted commits (merge commits) and a lack of specific details about the features they built. Overall, Bug Buster's team built a functional and promising financial intelligence platform that could be further developed and tested.
